measurement_note: Size is n, the number of buildings. Each tier alternates a free drop to height 1 with descending positive climbs; ladders scale as n/8 and a large brick budget ensures every transition is processed. The Accepted bounded min-heap and an independent heap-replacement implementation take O(n log(l+1)) time. A correct implementation that linearly searches the retained ladder climbs for the smallest allocation takes O(n*l), completes all 8/32/128 tiers, and is rejected only by scaling.
